steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content:
Rewrite $8x + 2y = 0$ in slope-intercept form:
$$2y = -8x$$
$$y = -4x$$
The slope of line $s$ is $m_s = -4$.

steps.title: Find the slope of line t using perpendicularity
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals. If line $s$ has slope $-4$, then line $t$ has slope:
$$m_t = -\frac{1}{-4} = \frac{1}{4}$$

steps.title: Use the slope formula with the two points to find k
steps.content:
Line $t$ passes through $(6, 0)$ and $(k, \frac{5}{4})$. The slope between these points must equal $\frac{1}{4}$:
$$\frac{\frac{5}{4} - 0}{k - 6} = \frac{1}{4}$$
Simplify:
$$\frac{5/4}{k - 6} = \frac{1}{4}$$
Cross-multiply:
$$\frac{5}{4} \cdot 4 = k - 6$$
$$5 = k - 6$$
$$k = 11$$
